new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a non-alcoholic beverage that is a tonic preparation or food supplement, potentially based on plant or herbal extracts and potentially containing added vitamins and/or minerals, as described in the explanatory notes to CN Chapter 22.

Non-alcoholic beverages of CN heading 2202 99 19: other non-alcoholic beverages not elsewhere specified or included, covering tonic preparations (food supplements) based on plant/herbal extracts and possibly containing added vitamins and/or minerals, classified under the 'other' subheading of CN 2202 99.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the three main discriminating criteria from the legal text: (1) non-alcoholic beverage character (NonAlcoholicBeverageQuality), (2) the tonic/food-supplement nature based on plant/herbal extracts with possible vitamins/minerals (TonicPreparationFoodSupplementQuality), and (3) exclusion from the flavoured/sweetened water subheading 2202 10 00 (complement of FlavoredOrSweetenedWaterQuality). However, coverage is partial because: (a) the exact distinction from sibling code 22029911 (which covers other beverages under 2202 99) cannot be fully expressed without a dedicated quality for the 22029911 sibling; (b) the plant/herbal extract ingredient basis and the vitamin/mineral content are described textually but cannot be expressed as fine-grained OWL restrictions without introducing ingredient-type properties not yet in the TBox; (c) the reference to the Chapter 22 explanatory note general paragraph 2 is purely administrative and not expressible as an OWL restriction. Overall structural placement and primary discriminating qualities are covered; ingredient-level granularity is not.
